dream.title: Wolf Dreams
dream.context: dreaming of a wolf: instinct, the outsider, threat or guidance from the wild part of the psyche; the pack (belonging) versus the lone wolf (independence or isolation).

dream.title: Vomiting in a Dream
dream.context: vomiting: a forceful need to expel something toxic — a feeling, a situation, words held back. Rejection of what no longer agrees with you; release that can't stay polite.

dream.title: Plane Crash Dreams
dream.context: a plane crash: a high-stakes plan or ambition feared to be failing; loss of control over something you can't personally steer. Distinct from missing a flight (lost opportunity).

dream.title: Driving Dreams
dream.context: driving — especially brakes failing, losing control, or sitting in the back seat: how much agency you feel over your own direction in life, and who is really 'driving' your choices.

dream.title: Snake in the House Dream
dream.h1: Dreams About a Snake in Your House
dream.context: a snake inside the home: a hidden threat or tension within your private or family life — or inner self — something unsettling that has gotten into your safe space. Distinct from snakes outdoors.

dream.title: Demon Dreams
dream.context: a demon: confronting a shadow aspect, guilt, fear, or an oppressive force; the felt sense of evil, and the sleep-paralysis imagery that often accompanies it. Psychological, not literal.

dream.title: Dreaming of a Miscarriage
dream.context: dreaming of a miscarriage: fear of loss around something nascent — a plan, a hope, a relationship — surfacing as anxiety, not prophecy. Handle with real compassion.

dream.title: Crying in a Dream
dream.context: crying: a release of feeling that may be suppressed while awake; grief, relief, or a long-held need finally surfacing where the waking guard is down.

dream.title: Owl Dreams
dream.context: dreaming of an owl: seeing what's hidden, intuition and wisdom — or, in some traditions, a warning. The owl as night-sight, knowing what others miss in the dark.

dream.title: Bull Dreams
dream.context: dreaming of a bull: powerful drive, stubbornness, fertility and aggression; a force charging at you, or your own untamed energy demanding direction.

dream.title: Eagle Dreams
dream.context: dreaming of an eagle: vision, ambition, freedom and a higher perspective; rising above a situation — or the sense of being watched from above.

dream.title: Hair Falling Out in a Dream
dream.context: hair falling out: anxiety about appearance, ageing, control, or vitality; a loss of confidence or 'face'. Distinct from a haircut, which is a chosen change.

dream.title: Being Kidnapped in a Dream
dream.context: being kidnapped: feeling powerless, controlled, or taken somewhere against your will in waking life; a loss of autonomy, or being pulled off your own path by another's force.

dream.title: Cockroach Dreams
dream.context: cockroaches: resilience and survival, but also disgust, neglect, or a problem that keeps returning no matter how hard you try to be rid of it.

dream.title: Dreaming of Divorce
dream.context: dreaming of divorce: a separation being processed — from a person, a belief, or a phase; fear of, or quiet readiness for, an ending. Not a prediction about your relationship.

dream.title: Hiding in a Dream
dream.context: hiding: avoidance, a part of yourself or a truth you don't want seen; safety-seeking, shame, or a genuine need for space and protection.

symbol.title: Lighthouse Symbolism
symbol.context: the lighthouse: guidance through darkness, hope, safe return, and steadfastness amid storms; the fixed point that helps others find their way.

symbol.title: Zodiac Symbolism
symbol.context: the zodiac as a symbolic system: the wheel of twelve, archetypes of character and fate, the heavens mapped onto human temperament. An overview, not a horoscope.

symbol.title: Dharma Wheel Symbolism
symbol.context: the dharma wheel (dharmachakra): the Buddha's teaching set in motion, the Noble Eightfold Path in its spokes, and the turning of cosmic law.

symbol.title: Acorn Symbolism
symbol.context: the acorn: latent potential — 'mighty oaks from little acorns' — patience and quiet strength; Norse and Victorian traditions of the acorn as luck and protection.

symbol.title: Palm Tree Symbolism
symbol.context: the palm: victory, peace, paradise and resilience; Palm Sunday and triumphal entry, and the palm that bends in the storm but does not break.

symbol.title: Peace Sign Symbolism
symbol.context: the peace sign: its origin in the nuclear-disarmament (CND) movement, unity and nonviolence; distinct from the two-finger peace 'V' gesture and its separate history.

symbol.title: Trident Symbolism
symbol.context: the trident: Poseidon and Neptune and the power of the sea, Shiva's trishula and the three gunas; authority over the elements held in three prongs.

symbol.title: Caduceus Symbolism
symbol.context: the caduceus versus the Rod of Asclepius: Hermes, commerce and messengers (two snakes, wings) against true medicine (one snake); the common and revealing mix-up.

symbol.title: Valknut Symbolism
symbol.context: the valknut: Odin, the slain, and the bind of three interlocking triangles; its Norse archaeological context and a careful note on its modern appropriations.

number.title: 1551 Meaning
number.context: 1551 as an angel number: change (5) framed by beginnings (1); a transition that opens a new path, freedom pursued with intention.

number.title: 1661 Meaning
number.context: 1661 as an angel number: home and harmony (6) framed by beginnings (1); nurturing a fresh start, balancing responsibility with self.

number.title: 1771 Meaning
number.context: 1771 as an angel number: spiritual insight (7) framed by beginnings (1); a new chapter guided by inner wisdom, the seeker stepping forward.

number.title: 1881 Meaning
number.context: 1881 as an angel number: abundance and power (8) framed by beginnings (1); material renewal and karmic balance entering a new cycle.

number.title: 1991 Meaning
number.context: 1991 as an angel number: completion (9) framed by beginnings (1); an ending that seeds a new beginning, humanitarian purpose coming full circle.
